questions.content: The equipment that processes data in order to create information is called the ____.
In most cases, the word "software" is interchangeable with the word ____.
"Background" software that helps the computer manage its own internal resources is called ____.
Desktop, laptop, tablet computers, and mobile devices are classified as ____.
The most widely used personal computers are ____.
A microcomputer that is smaller, lighter, and less powerful than a notebook, and which has a touch-sensitive screen, is called a ____.
____ are the most important part of any information system.
The type of media that uses laser technology to store data and programs is ____.
The Internet is the largest ____ in the world.
In an information system, the rules or guidelines for people to follow are called ____.
____ is the component of the information system that is described as the equipment that processes data to create information.
The component of the information system that is described as raw, unprocessed facts, including text, numbers, images, and sounds, is called ____.
____ software is a collection of programs that helps the computer manage its own resources and enables application software to interact with computer hardware.
The ____ is the continuing development of the Internet that allows everyday objects embedded with electronic devices to send and receive data over the Internet.
Application software can be categorized as either general-purpose, specialized, or ____ apps.
Software is another name for ____.
____ computers are not nearly as powerful as supercomputers, but are capable of great processing speeds and data storage and occupy specially wired, air-conditioned rooms.
The five types of personal computers are: desktops, laptops, tablets, smartphones, and ____.
____ are cell phones with wireless connections to the Internet.
____ storage devices do not lose data or programs after the electrical power is turned off.
____ use(s) rigid metallic platters to store programs and very large data files.
A(n) ____ file might contain audience handouts and speaker notes in addition to electronic slides.
Increased use of mobile or ____ communication devices and cloud computing has been termed "the connectivity revolution".
A(n) ____ is a communications system connecting two or more computers.
____ computing uses the Internet and the web to shift many computer activities from a user's computer to computers on the Internet.
The parts of an information system are people, procedures, software, hardware, data, and the Internet. ( )
The purpose of software is to convert data into information. ( )
Software is another name for a program or programs. ( )
To be an effective and efficient computer user, you need to recognize the role of information technology in your personal and professional life. ( )
Application software is "background" software which helps the computer manage its own internal resources. ( )
System software is a collection of programs. ( )
Antivirus software is a Windows utility that is unnecessary as long as you are careful browsing the Internet. ( )
System software might be described as end-user software. ( )
A word processor is an example of system software. ( )
Another name for a midrange computer is “server”. ( )
Insurance companies are most likely to use personal computers to process information about millions of policyholders. ( )
Smartphones are a type of personal computer that do not need input, output, processing, or storage. ( )
Two important components of the system unit are the keyboard and the hard disk. ( )
A system unit consists of the keyboard and the monitor. ( )
RAM will retain data when the computer is turned off. ( )
The most common input devices are the mouse and the keyboard. ( )
Secondary storage will retain data when the computer is turned off. ( )
Solid-state storage uses rigid metallic platters to store data using the magnetic charges on the disk's surface. ( )
Four common types of files are document files, worksheet files, database files, and presentation files. ( )
Highly structured and organized data is stored in database files. ( )
Launched in 1969 as a U.S.-funded project that developed a national computer network, the Internet was initially called ____.
The Web was introduced in ____.
The Internet is a ____ made up of wires, cables, satellites, and rules for exchanging information between computers connected to the network.
Introduced in 1991, the web provides a(n) ____ interface to Internet resources.
The most common way to access the Internet is through ____ .
A web page typically contains ____, which contains the formatting instructions for displaying the web page.
For browsers to connect to resources on the Internet, the location of the resources must be specified through addresses called ____.
In the URL "http://www.simnetonline.com", ".com" is the ____.
The ____ compares your entry against its database and returns the result, a list of sites on that topic.
In a(n) ____ sellers post descriptions of products at a website and buyers submit bids electronically.
Google Chrome is an example of a ____.
The protocol ____ is used for web traffic and is one of the most widely used Internet protocols.
In “http://www.simnetonline.com”, the underlined section is the ____ name.
____ e-mail systems do not require an e-mail program to be installed on your computer.
Unsolicited e-mail is called ____.
A(n) ____ allows an individual or company to share posts with an audience; however, they are designed to be used with mobile devices and limit the size of posts.
A ____ is a website specially designed to allow visitors to use their browser to add, edit, or delete the site’s content.
A(n) ____ engine is a specialized program that assists you in locating information on the web and the Internet.
An Internet ____ is a collection of utility programs designed to maintain your security and privacy while you are on the web.
Blog postings are ____ and arranged with the newest item first.
The Internet connects millions of computers and resources throughout the world via wires, cables, and satellite. ( )
The Internet and the Web are the same. ( )
The Apple Health application is a Web 3.0 application. ( )
In addition to an Internet connection, users need browser software to easily display web resources. ( )
In order to connect to a website, the browser must know only the site's domain name. ( )
JavaScript is a language often used within HTML documents to trigger interactive features. ( )
Cascading Style Sheets (CSS) are files inserted into an HTML document that control the appearance of web pages including layout, colors, and fonts.
PHP is a language often used within HTML documents to improve a website’s interactivity. ( )
Client-based e-mail accounts require a special program called an e-mail client to be installed on your computer. ( )
Attachments to e-mail messages can be a document or an image. ( )
CAN-SPAM requirements have proven to be more effective than spam blockers at preventing unwanted e-mail. ( )
Instant messaging allows text only. ( )
Cloud computing is an example of a social networking site. ( )
LinkedIn is a business-oriented social networking site that you should visit if you are looking for professional employment. ( )
Instagram, a popular microblogging site, is designed to share images and videos posts, with little to no written content.( )
Wikis allow many individuals to edit the site's content.( )
All information found on the web through search tools can be considered as factual. ( )
One of the greatest challenges for the growth of e-commerce is the development of fast, secure, and reliable payment methods for buying goods. ( )
News feed is the first page you see after logging into a social networking site. ( )
Cloud computing has the potential to free end users from owning, maintaining, and storing software and data. ( )
Uploading is the process of copying a file from a website to your computer. ( )
System software can be described as end user software and is used to accomplish a variety of tasks. ( )
Application software includes mobile apps, general-purpose applications, and specialized applications.( )
Microsoft Access is an example of general-purpose application software.( )
A window is a rectangular area that can contain a document, a program, or a message.( )
Microsoft applications use an interface with traditional menus and toolbars. ( )
In a Ribbon GUI, a gallery displays the additional options. ( )
In a Ribbon GUI, tabs are used to divide the ribbon into major activity areas. ( )
Microsoft Word cannot be used to create simple web pages. ( )
Microsoft Word can identify incomplete sentences, awkward wording, and incorrect punctuation. ( )
Functions, formulas, charts, and what-if analysis are common features of database management systems. ( )
The last worksheet in a workbook is often a summary of the preceding worksheets. ( )
What-if analysis is a very powerful and simple tool to test the effects of different assumptions in a spreadsheet. ( )
A database is a collection of related data. ( )
In a database, primary keys are often used to link tables. ( )
Worksheets are only used for limited types of applications in certain professions. ( )
Presentation software is an excellent tool to communicate a message and to persuade people. ( )
Desktop publishing programs focus on page design and layout and provide greater flexibility for this than word processors. ( )
Image editors are used to create and edit vector images. ( )
Illustration programs are specialized graphics programs for editing bitmap or raster images. ( )
Web authoring programs are used to create sophisticated commercial websites. ( )
Video apps are a favorite way to watch movie trailers and video blogs. ( )
Cloud suites are stored on your hard drive and are available anywhere you can access the Internet. ( )
Many apps are written for a particular type of mobile device and will not run on other types. ( )
____ provide additional information and request user input.
Most general-purpose applications use a(n) ____ that displays graphical elements called icons to represent familiar objects.
The electronic equivalent of a file cabinet is a ____.
____ are used to edit videos to enhance quality and appearance.
Specialized and powerful programs, called ____, are typically used to create specialized commercial sites.
Web authoring programs that allow you to build a page without interacting directly with HTML code are known as ____.
____ make up the basic structure of a relational database with columns containing field data and rows containing record information.
As opposed to general-purpose applications, which are widely used in nearly every discipline, ____ applications are more narrowly focused.
____ software is end user software designed to accomplish a variety of tasks.
In a Ribbon GUI, ____ are used to divide the ribbon into major activity areas.
____ are used to create text-based documents.
____ organize, analyze, and graph numeric data such as budgets and financial reports.
A ____ is a collection of related data—the electronic equivalent of a file cabinet.
____ are used to quickly create a presentation by providing predesigned styles and layouts as well as suggested content based on the type you choose.
____ are programs that combine a variety of visual objects to create attractive, visually interesting presentations.
____ programs allow you to mix text and graphics to create publications of professional quality.
Digital photographs consist of thousands of dots, or ____, that form images.
____ images are made up of geometric shapes or objects.
Creating a website is called ____ .
____ apps are add-on programs for a variety of devices including cell phones and tablets.
A(n) ____ store store is where you can download applications to work with your mobile, laptop, and desktop devices either for a nominal fee or free of charge.
Office suites are also known as ____ suites.
Cloud suites are stored at a ____ on the Internet, rather than on your microcomputer.
Common features of most applications include ____, ____, spell checkers, grammar checkers, find and replace, character effects, and editing options.
To make your presentation more professional and eye-catching, you select a ____, built-in sets of colors, fonts, and effects that can be quickly applied to your entire presentation.
After collecting data, the next step is to structure the worksheet, followed by inserting numeric entries and applying formulas and ____ to perform calculations.
Most operating systems support the ability to switch between different applications. This is called ____.
The operating system is often referred to as the software environment or software ____.
____ programs provide a quick and easy way to search or examine an entire computer system to help you find specific applications, data, or other files.
____ accept commands through text or speech to allow intuitive interaction with your computer, cell phone, or tablet and coordinate personal data across multiple applications.
Every computer has an operating system, and every operating system performs a variety of functions including ____, ____, and ____.
questions.answer: managing resources; providing user interface; running applications

questions.content: ____ programs help solve the problem of running out of storage space by providing lists of application programs, stored videos, and other program files so that you can eliminate unused applications or archive large files elsewhere.
Most ____ are accompanied by several common utility programs, including a search program, a storage management program, and a backup program.
____ software consists of operating systems, utilities, device drivers, and language translators.
A(n) ____ converts the programming instructions written by programmers into a language that the computer understands and processes.
The ____ system coordinates computer resources, provides the user interface, and runs applications.
____ are graphic representations for a program, type of file, or function.
Cell phone operating systems are a type of ____ operating system.
____ operating systems are used to control and coordinate computers that are linked together.
Windows Server is an example of a(n) ____ operating system.
____, also known as ____, are used in cell phones and tablets.
____ operating systems control a single desktop or laptop computer.
The ____ is often referred to as the software environment or software platform.
____ is an operating system widely used by servers on the web, mainframe computers, and very powerful personal computers.
____ refers to the process whereby a single physical computer can support multiple operating systems.
____ or diagnostic programs recognize and correct problems.
Antivirus programs, backup programs, and virtual assistants are all examples of ____ programs.
____ are specialized programs designed to make computing easier.
In a virtualized environment, the operating system of each virtual machine is known as the ____ operating system.
____ programs guard your computer system against viruses or other damaging programs that can invade your computer system.
System software is a single program.( )
System software cannot handle technical details without user intervention.()
Starting or restarting a computer is called tasking the system.( )
Many operating systems now include voice assist tools that allow a user to directly issue commands. ( )
In a network environment, the network server computer coordinates all communications between the other computers. ( )
A standard desktop computer comes with an embedded operating system. ( )
The operating system is often referred to as the software platform. ( )
Cell phones generally include a network operating system. ( )
Because macOS 12 Monterey market share is so large, more application programs are developed to run under macOS 12 than any other operating system. ( )
Linux is a popular and powerful alternative to the Windows operating system. ( )
When a single physical computer runs a special program known as virtualization software, it operates as though it were two or more separate and independent computers, known as virtual machines. ( )
Google’s Chrome OS integrates with web servers using cloud computing and storage to run applications and to perform other functions. ( )
Based on the macOS, Android is designed for Apple’s iPhone and iPad. ( )
Antivirus programs guard your computer system against viruses or other damaging programs that can invade your computer system. ( )
Utility suites come with one program only. ( )
There are several ways in which you can "catch" a computer virus. ( )
Programs such as word processors and spreadsheets are known as utilities. ( )
Backup programs can identify and remove unused files and applications. ( )
Antivirus programs, backup programs, and virtual assistants are all examples of utility programs. ( )
In a virtualized environment, the operating system of each virtual machine is known as the host operating system. ( )
Backup programs guard your computer system against viruses or other damaging programs that can invade your computer system. ( )
A byte is formed by a group of ____.
The system board is also known as the ____.
On the motherboard, the connection points for chips are referred to as ____.
Tiny circuit boards etched onto squares of sandlike material, called silicon, are called ____.
Integrated circuits typically are mounted on ____, which are then plugged into thesystem board.
Pathways that support communication among the various electronic components on the system board are called ____.
In most personal computer systems, the CPU is contained on a single chip called the ____.
A 32-bit-word computer can access ____.
The processing speed of a microprocessor is typically represented by its ____.
For multicore processors to be used effectively, computers must understand how to divide tasks into parts that can be distributed across each core - an operation called ____.
Frequently accessed information is held in ____.
There are four commonly used units of measurement to describe memory capacity, ____(MB), ____(GB),____(TB) and ____(PB).
questions.answer: megabyte / Megabyte; gigabyte / Gigabyte; terabyte / Terabyte; petabyte / Petabyte

questions.content: Most desktop and laptop computers come with a standard set of ports for connecting ____, such as a monitor and keyboard.
Laptops typically use ____ that are typically located outside the system unit.
The ____, also known as the system chassis, is a container that houses most of the electronic components that make up a computer system.
____, also known as mini notebooks, are lighter and thinner with longer battery life than most laptops.
The most widely used character encoding standard today is ____.
The ____ system consists of only two digits —0 and 1.
A ____ is the number of bits that can be accessed at one time by the CPU.
____ operations consist of making comparisons.
A(n) ____-bit-word computer can access eight bytes of information at a time.
____ allow a single computer to run two or more operations at the same time.
____ memory improves processing by acting as a temporary high-speed holding area between the memory and the CPU.
____ memory uses the hard drive to substitute for RAM.
____ cards are used to connect a computer to anetwork.
____ allow computers to be connected without cables.
Network interface cards (NIC) are connected through ____ on the systemboard.
____ buses connect the CPU to memory on the system board.
The number of bits that can travel simultaneously down a bus is known as the bus ____.
____ ports are the standard high-speed networking ports for many of today's computers.
____ ports provide high-speed connections to specialized devices such ascamcorders and storage devices.
____ ports are audiovisual ports typically used to connect large monitors. They are popular on gaming computers with high-end graphics cards.
____ ports are a high-speed version of USB-C ports.
____ are audiovisual ports typically used to connect large monitors.
Desktop computers have a(n)____ unit, located within the system unit, which plugs into a standard wall outlet, converts AC to DC, and provides the power to drive all the system unit components.
The system unit, also known as the system chassis, is a container that houses most of theelectronic components that make up a computer system. ( )
ASCII supports languages such as Chinese and Japanese. ( )
The ALU tells the rest of the computer system how to carry out a program's instructions. ( )
The control unit directs the movement of electronic signals between the memory and the ALU. ( )
A 64-bit-word computer can access 8 bytes at a time. ( )
Parallel processing requires a computer to perform one complete task before beginning the next task. ( )
RAM is considered temporary, or volatile storage. ( )
Virtual memory is stored on a RAM chip. ( )
Read-only memory (ROM) chips have information stored in them by the manufacturer. ( )
Graphics cards connect the system board to secondary storage. ( )
The number of bits that can travel simultaneously down a bus is known as the word size. ( )
A bus is a pathway for bits representing data and instructions. ( )
DVI ports provide connections to digital monitors. ( )
USB ports are used to connect keyboards, mice, printers, storage devices, and a variety of specialty devices. ( )
FireWire ports are used to connect keyboards, mice, printers, and storage devices. ( )

Cables are used to connect exterior devices to the system unit via the ports. One end of the cable is attached to the device, and the other end has a connector that is attached to a matching connector on the port. ( )
DP are an audiovisual port typically used to connect large monitors. ( )
HDMI ports make it possible to use a computer as a video jukebox or an HD video recorder.( )
Computers require direct current (DC) to power their electronic components and torepresent data and instructions. ( )
____ are hardware used to translate data and information that people understand into a form that the system unit can process.
____ is any data or instructions that are entered into a computer.
A keyboard key, like Caps Lock, which turns a feature on or off is called a ____ key.
____ perform an action when held down in combination with another key.
____-input devices convert sounds into a form that can be processed by the system unit.
The most widely used audio-input device is the ____.
The series of dots that form the image on a monitor are called ____.
Printers connected to the Internet that provide printing services to others on the Internet are called ____.
____ are used primarily on cell phones and tablets.
The mouse, game controllers, and touch screens are all types of ____ devices.
Flatbed, document, and portable are types of ____ that accept documents and convert them into machine-readable form.
____ are used to control game actions by users varying the pressure, speed, and direction of a control stick.
Bar code readers are either handheld ____ or ____,which contain ____ that scan bar codes.
questions.answer: wand readers / platform scanners; photo-electric cells / photoelectric cells

questions.content: The most common card reader is the ____ card reader which reads encoded information stored on a thin magnetic strip located on the back of the card.

____ tags are tiny chips that can be embedded into almost anything and contain information used to track and locate lost pets; to monitor production and update inventory; to record prices, product descriptions, and locations of retail items.
____ recognition is often used to score standardized multiple-choice tests.
____ are specialized digital video cameras that capture images and send them to a computer for broadcast over the Internet.
____ printers use a technology similar to that used in a photocopying machine.
____ printers are widely used with ATMs.
____ printers are printers connected to the Internet that provide printing services toothers on the Internet.
____ are special-purpose printers that use output from graphics tablets or other graphical input devices that are typically found in architectural and engineering environments.
____ combine the capabilities of a scanner, printer, fax, and copy machine.
____ is the distance between pixels on a monitor.
Virtual reality (VR) strives to create a virtual or ____ experience by using specialized hardware that includes headgear and gloves.
The most widely used input devices are monitors, printers, and keyboards. ( )
Combination keys perform an action when held down in combination with another key.( )
Handwriting recognition software translates handwritten notes into a form that the systemunit can process. ( )
A touch screen is classified as a special type of scanning device. ( )
A stylus interacts with the computer through handwriting recognition software. ( )
Multitouch screens allow for interaction such as rotating graphical objects on the screenwith your hand. ( )
Optical scanners recognize individual letters or images. ( )
A magnetic card reader does not require the card to actually make contact with the reader.( )
An MICR device senses the presence or absence of a mark, such as a pencil mark. ( )
The monitor size is measured by the diagonal length of the viewing area. ( )
Output devices are any hardware used to provide or to create output. ( )
Flexible screens allow digital devices to display images on non-flat surfaces. ( )
Digital or interactive whiteboards display a computer’s desktop and can be controlled usinga special pen or a finger. ( )
E-ink produces images that reflect light like ordinary paper, making the display easy to read.( )
Resolution for a printer is a measure of the clarity of images produced, measured in dpi(dots per inch). ( )
Shared laser printers typically support color, are more expensive, and are used by a group ofpeople. ( )
Cloud printers provide printing services to users without access to the Internet. ( )
Thermal printers use heat elements to produce images on heat-sensitive paper and are usedat ATMs and gasoline pumps. ( )
Wireless audio-output connections typically use Bluetooth technology. ( )
Multifunctional devices (MFDs) are used to capture as well asplayback recorded sounds. ( )
One particular type of RSI, carpal tunnel syndrome, found among heavy computer users,consists of damage to nerves and tendons in the hands. ( )
Robots use cameras, microphones, and other sensors as inputs; outputs can be as complexas exploring damaged nuclear reactors to as simple as taking a photo. ( )
Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAV) are too expensive for anything but military budgets.( )
LCD monitors produce better-quality images, are slimmer, and are more environmentally friendly as they require less power and use fewer toxic chemicals. ( )
On a hard disk each track is divided into invisible wedge-shaped sections called ____.
Storage that retains its data after the power is turned off is referred to as ____ storage.
The process of accessing information from secondary storage devices is referred to as ____.
The ____ of a secondary storage device indicates how much data the storage medium can hold.
____ speed is the measure of the amount of time required by a storage device to retrieve data and programs.
Tracks are divided into invisible wedge-shaped sections called ____.
____ improves hard-disk performance by anticipating data needs and reducing time to access data from secondary storage.
An ____ hard disk is typically connected to a USB or Thunderbolt port.
File ____ expands compressed files.
USB drives are also known as ____ drives.
____ memory cards are a form of a solid-state storage device widely used in cell phones, laptops, and GPS navigation systems.
With ____, or online storage, you can access your files from any computer with an Internet connection.
Media are the actual physical material that holds the data and programs. ( )
The process of saving information to a secondary storage device is referred to as embedding. ( )
Secondary storage provides temporary or volatile storage. ( )
Hard disks use tracks, sectors, and cylinders to store and organize files. ( )
Typically, hybrid drives use SSD to store the operating system and applications and hard disks to store videos, music, and documents. ( )
Network hard disk drives exist local to the system unit, either within the system unit or nearby. ( )
External hard drives are used primarily to complement an internal hard disk. ( )
External hard disks use different technology than internal hard disks. ( )
Disk caching improves hard-disk performance by compressing files. ( )
Disk caching uses a combination of hardware and software. ( )
RAID connects several inexpensive hard-disk drives to one another. ( )
File compression is only available on hard-disk drives. ( )
CD-ROMs are often used to archive data and to record music downloaded from the Internet. ( )
DVDs and Blu-rays have largely replaced CDs as the standard optical disc. ( )
Blu-ray discs are designed for high definition and have a far greater capacity than DVDs. ( )
Facebook is an example of cloud storage. ( )
If you have used Google Drive Docs to create a word processing document, you have used cloud computing. ( )
In order to access your documents and files that are stored in the cloud, you must have an Internet connection. ( )
Mass storage devices are specialized high-capacity secondary storage devices designed to store large amounts of data for an organization. ( )
File servers are appropriate devices to use in an enterprise storage system. ( )
Microwave communication uses high-frequency ____.
____ is a term relating to sending data to a satellite.
A service that uses existing phone lines to provide high-speed connections is called ____.
The capacity of a communication channel is measured in ____.
Every computer on the Internet has a unique numeric address called a(n) ____.
____ is the process of breaking down information sent or transmitted across the Internet into small parts.
The ____ is the central node that coordinates the flow of data by sending messages directly between the sender and receiver nodes.
____ is a high-frequency transmission medium that has a single solid-copper core.
____ is the name of the process of converting from analog to digital.
The range of Wi-Fi networks using microwave connections is being extended over greater distances using a wireless technology known as ____.
____ transmits data as pulses of light through tiny tubes of glass.
A ____ is a network where the nodes are in close physical proximity to each other.
A network ____ is a device that allows one LAN to be linked to other LANs or to larger networks.
A LAN set up for a household's personal use is called a ____ network.
____ are countrywide and worldwide networks.
A ____ area network is a type of wireless network that works within your immediate surroundings to connect cell phones to headsets, controllers to game systems, and soon.
A ____ network system uses central servers to coordinate and supply services to other nodes on the network.
A(n) ____ is a private network within an organization that is designed to resemble the Internet.
An ____ is a private network that connects more than one organization.
In a client/____ network, the server provides access to resources and services.
A ____ network topology does not use a specific physical layout but requires that each node have more than one connection to the other nodes.
A hierarchical network topology in which each device is connected to a central node, either directly or through one or more other devices, is also called a ____ network.
In a wireless LAN, all communications pass through the network's centrally located wireless access point or ____ station.
____ describes how a network is arranged and how resources arecoordinated and shared.
Most firewalls include a special computer called a ____ server to manage communications between the internal networks and the outside world.
Connection devices are the actual connecting or transmission medium that carries the message. ( )
Standard telephone lines use coaxial cables. ( )
Fiber-optic cable transmits data as pulses of light through tiny tubes of glass. ( )
Modulation is the name of the process of converting from digital to analog. ( )
TCP/IP protocol involves identifying, sending, and receiving devices and breaking information into small parts for transmission across the Internet. ( )
A prototype is a set of communication rules for the exchange of information. ( )
Packetization refers to breaking information into small parts for transmission across the Internet. ( )
In a network environment, a client provides resources for the servers. ( )
Network operating systems (NOS) control and coordinate the activities of all computers and other devices on a network. ( )
WANs are widely used by organizations to link personal computers and to share printers and other resources. ( )
A network gateway can be used to connect the LAN of one office group to the LAN of another office group. ( )
The most common standard in LANs is called Ethernet. ( )
A MAN is a network that is frequently used as links between office buildings that are located throughout a city. ( )
Network architecture describes how a computer network is configured and what topologies and strategies are employed. ( )
The client/server network strategy can handle very large networks efficiently. ( )
In a peer-to-peer network, only the central host computer supplies the resources; the other computers request resources from this host. ( )
With respect to peer-to-peer network, there is an abundant amount of powerful management software that monitors the network activities. ( )
A firewall is a specialized technology designed to protect an organization's network against external threats. ( )
Intrusion detection systems can recognize signs of a network attack and disable access before an intruder can do damage. ( )
People that collect, analyze, and sell personal data are called ____.
Small data files that are deposited on a user's hard disk when they visit a website are called ____.
Programs used to secretly record an individual's activities on the Internet are called ____.
Free computer games and free screen saver programs are typical carriers of ____.
The most significant concerns for implementation of computer technology involve ____, security, and ethics.
A ____ acts as a security buffer between a corporation's private network and allexternal networks.
____ is the illegal assumption of someone's identity for the purposes of economic gain.
A ____ is a computer criminal who creates and distributes malicious programs ormalware.
____ are invisible images or HTML code hidden within a web page or e-mail message and are used to transmit information without your knowledge.
____ are programs that migrate through networks and operating systems and attach themselves to various other programs and databases.
A deceptive act, using the Internet, to trick people into spending their money and time for no gain is called an Internet ____.
____ attempts to trick Internet users into thinking a fake but official-looking websiteor e-mail is legitimate.
____ is the use of the Internet to send or post content intended to hurt orembarrass another person.
____ is the process of coding information to make it unreadable except to those who have a special piece of information known as a key.
____ scanning devices use fingerprints and iris scanners to identify individuals.
____ encrypt connections between company networks and remote users, such as workers connecting from home, creating a secure virtual connection to a company LAN across the Internet.
As a security buffer, all electronic communications coming into and leaving a corporation must be evaluated by the ____.
____ is representing some other person’s work and ideas as your own withoutgiving credit to the original source.
The essential element that controls how computers are used is a law. ( )
Information systems consist of people, procedures, software, hardware, data, and theInternet. ( )
Your digital footprint can reveal more than you might wish to make public and have animpact beyond what you might imagine. ( )
Identity theft and mistaken identity are the same. ( )
Most browsers offer a privacy mode which ensures that your browsing activity is notrecorded on your hard disk. ( )
A tracking cookie is generally used by an advertising company affiliated with a website youhave visited to keep track of your web activity as you move from one site tothe next. ( )
Web bugs are usually hidden within the HTML code for a web page or in an e-mailmessage as an invisible image. ( )
It is against the law for keyloggers tobe deposited on your hard drive by the company youwork for. ( )
Spy removal programs are designed to detect and remove various types of privacy threats. ( )
The archiving and search features of the web make your online identity availableindefinitely. ( )
Computer security focuses on protecting information, hardware, and software from unauthorized use as well as preventing or limiting the damage from intrusions, sabotage, andnatural disasters. ( )
Posting personal information such as your birth date, family member names, and homeaddress, does not increase your risk of identity theft. ( )
